2606.06787.title: AdMem: Advanced Memory for Task-solving Agents
2606.06787.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise as tool-using agents but remain limited in long-horizon tasks that require remembering, organizing, and reusing knowledge. Prior memory approaches aim to resolve the situation, but mainly focus on storing factual information. Recent work on procedural memory improves task reuse, yet often reduces to replaying past successes without addressing failure cases or online scalability. We introduce a unified and automatic memory framework that integrates semantic, episodic, and procedural memory in a bi-level design combining short-term and long-term stores. A multi-agent architecture with actor, memory, and critic agents enables automatic memory generation, reward annotation, and adaptive retrieval. Long-term memory is managed through reward-based evaluation, merging, and pruning, ensuring scalability and continual improvement. Experiments across various environments show that our approach improves robustness and success on long multi-turn tasks compared to existing baselines. This work highlights the importance of comprehensive, adaptive memory for advancing LLM-based agents.
2606.06787.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）作為工具使用代理顯示出潛力，但在需要記憶、組織和重用知識的長期任務中仍然有限。<lb/>先前的記憶方法旨在解決這一情況，但主要集中於儲存事實信息。<lb/>最近在程序性記憶方面的研究改善了任務重用，但往往簡化為重播過去的成功，而未解決失敗案例或在線可擴展性問題。<lb/>我們提出了一個統一的自動記憶框架，整合了語義記憶、情節記憶和程序性記憶，採用雙層設計，結合短期和長期存儲。<lb/>一個多代理架構，包含行為者、記憶和評估代理，使自動記憶生成、獎勵註釋和自適應檢索成為可能。<lb/>長期記憶通過基於獎勵的評估、合併和修剪進行管理，確保可擴展性和持續改進。<lb/>在各種環境中的實驗表明，我們的方法在長多輪任務上相較於現有基準提高了穩健性和成功率。<lb/>這項工作突顯了全面、自適應記憶在推進基於LLM的代理中的重要性。
2606.06787.author: Runzhe Wang et.al.
2606.06787.authors: Runzhe Wang, Huilin Lu, Shengjie Liu, Li Dong, Jason Zhu
